assets.title: Canonical Core Foundation Asset
assets.purpose: Single source of truth for product identity and reasoning constraints. This bundle replaces multiple fragmented principle assets.
contractLines: - `phonowell`: drop assets in, make one wish, get one artifact
- promise: `You make a wish. We make it real.`
- each well has exactly one `goal-origin` asset
- AI drafts it, user can edit and confirm
- user wording is signal, not always final intent
- intent hypotheses stay explicit until convergence
- repeated high blast radius triggers asset partition refactor
- humans are not good at managing high project complexity directly
- system should absorb complexity and keep user interaction minimal
- user flow continuity is mandatory: ingest and basic editing must stay smooth and non-blocking
- generated artifacts should be minimal by default
- prefer mature existing assets/libraries/tools before custom from-scratch implementation
- reduce uncertainty by maximizing proven components
- artifact acceptance conditions are first-class assets
- acceptance contract must be explicit before verify can pass
guardrailLines: - no hidden decisions outside asset graph
- no generation before confirmed goal-origin
- no verify pass without explicit acceptance contract coverage
- no persistent high-coupling asset structure
- no unnecessary user-facing complexity
- no workflow step should block normal drop/edit/connect actions unless generation safety requires it
- no default from-scratch implementation when mature alternatives exist
keywords: You make a wish. We make it real.

assets.title: Canonical Legacy Archive Boundary Asset
assets.purpose: Single source of truth that `legacy` assets are archive-only and must not participate in active runtime execution.
contractLines: - legacy assets exist only for historical traceability
- legacy assets must not be loaded into active runtime state
- legacy assets must not be counted in active coverage or gate decisions
- runtime catalog, orchestration state, and UI default views use active assets only
- any replayed persisted state must filter out legacy drops and legacy relations
guardrailLines: - no generation based on legacy assets
- no verify evidence sourced from legacy assets
- no UI default surface should show legacy assets as active choices

assets.title: Canonical Acceptance Contract Asset
assets.purpose: Single source of truth for artifact acceptance conditions. This asset defines what "done" means and what must be verified before release.
guardrailLines: - no release candidate without explicit acceptance contract link
- no verify pass without acceptance coverage evidence
- no hidden acceptance criteria outside asset graph

assets.title: Canonical Execution Protocol Asset
assets.purpose: Single source of truth for how chat signals become executable asset graph state.
contractLines: - collect intent fragments, constraints, and references
- commit incoming drops immediately after intake
- ensure each new drop is visible in graph/UI before deeper analysis
- assign provisional metadata (`scope`, `domain`, `priority`) with explicit heuristic defaults unless a model-assisted path is separately recorded
- generate intent hypotheses and uncertainty flags
- prefer mature reusable assets before introducing custom build assets
- prefer deterministic code implementation to reduce uncertainty; delegate only LLM-only tasks to LLM
- detect and annotate conflicts after commit
- do not block normal editing by default
- run a mandatory pre-generation dry run
- if `fail`, generation is blocked until issues are re-routed and updated
- if `warn`, generation can continue with explicit visibility
- all unresolved user-facing questions must be closed in dry-run before entering generate
- closure check: goal-origin -> required capabilities -> candidate execution path
- acceptance check: acceptance contract bound and each acceptance item is checkable
- self-iteration check: self-management acceptance evidence paths are defined and runnable
- completeness check: required assets and critical links present
- conflict check: unresolved contradictions and severity
- reuse-first check: mature alternatives evaluated before custom implementation
- reverse validation: adversarial questions + boundary cases + failure-mode probes
- asset-clarity check: all active assets have clear `content`, `purpose`, and at least one explicit relation path
- design-health check: detect overlap, contradiction, redundancy, obvious errors, and low-ROI design choices
- `asset-clarity warn`: reserved for future confidence-scoped clarity heuristics; V1 does not emit it when all clarity counts are `0`
- planning/gap-check/generation use committed graph as source of truth
- generate stage is execution-only and must not ask new user questions
- system decides the next step after verify: back to `gap-check`, `analyze`, or controlled regenerate
- route decision must be evidence-backed and visible in run report
- verify pass requires acceptance coverage evidence linked to `drop-canon-acceptance-contract`
- verify pass requires at least one self-iteration evidence packet when self-management is in acceptance scope
- user override is triggered when any of the following is true:
- AI route bypasses a mandatory stage after `fail` (`gap-check` required before regenerate)
- same route repeats for `>=2` verify cycles with no reduction in `fail-count` or `warn-count`
- route conflicts with latest explicit user goal/constraint update
- route selects custom build while mature reusable option exists without evidence
- system can raise/lower `priority` (`p0|p1|p2`) after each verify cycle when evidence threshold is met
- updates are based on impact to goal-origin and current risk
- deferred lifecycle updates must record override-required reason
- user override is triggered when any of the following is true:
- AI promotes more than `2` drops to `p0` in one verify cycle
- AI demotes a drop that is upstream of unresolved `fail` items
- same drop changes priority back and forth within `2` consecutive cycles
- priority change has no evidence linked to goal impact or risk delta
- new small generated assets default to `scope=run-local` and `priority=p2`
- each micro asset must attach to a parent decision via `parent-drop-id`
- AI promotes micro assets only when they block critical path, show cross-module reuse, or carry high verification risk
- non-promoted micro assets are compressed into candidate summaries and archived after milestone
- AI handles summarization, organization, and most re-evaluation
- heuristics may handle summarization, organization, and re-evaluation when no model-assisted evidence is available
- UI exposes only essential controls in default mode
`goal-origin-init -> chat-intake -> ingest -> first-principles-modeling -> conflict-annotate -> analyze -> organize -> gap-check -> dry-run -> generate -> verify`
keywords: goal-origin-init -> chat-intake -> ingest -> first-principles-modeling -> conflict-annotate -> analyze -> organize -> gap-check -> dry-run -> generate -> verify

assets.title: Canonical Data Format Contract Asset
assets.purpose: Single source of truth for the standard phonowell data model and persistence format. This asset defines how active assets, runtime state, generation diffs, and verification evidence must be represented.
contractLines: - active runtime drops inherit the same normalized metadata shape
- source-of-truth assets must preserve `source-file` for traceability
- core categorical fields use explicit allowed values instead of free strings
- required constrained fields include at least:
- active project state persists as one top-level `state.json`
- generation diff must include asset additions, removals, changes, and relation deltas
- diff must expose changed well-level fields when goal, acceptance, definition of done, or constraints move
- canonical active asset metadata must be validated on load
- persisted runtime state must be normalized and validated before reuse
- schema files are implementation artifacts that derive from this contract, not a parallel source of truth
guardrailLines: - no active runtime entity with missing `owner`
- no free-form categorical values for core asset fields
- no persisted project state reload without contract normalization
- no generation diff that hides relation or well-field changes

assets.title: Canonical State Versioning Contract Asset
assets.purpose: Single source of truth for versioning and migration of phonowell persisted state.
contractLines: 2. state loading must support migration from older known versions
3. incompatible state shape changes must be expressed as explicit migrations
4. migration runs before runtime validation and state reuse
guardrailLines: - no silent breaking state shape change
- no persisted state reload without version-aware normalization

assets.title: Canonical Entity Schema Contract Asset
assets.purpose: Single source of truth for explicit schemas of phonowell entities instead of validating only the top-level container.
contractLines: 2. top-level state schema composes entity schemas
3. runtime validation must reject malformed entity records even if top-level state shape exists
guardrailLines: - no top-level-only schema gate
- no untyped entity payload hidden inside valid-looking state

assets.title: Canonical Relation Semantics Contract Asset
assets.purpose: Single source of truth for a tighter and more explicit relation vocabulary inside the phonowell asset graph.
contractLines: 2. relation meaning must stay stable across runtime, diff, gate, and UI
3. default auto-generated relations use the minimal valid semantic instead of a generic catch-all
guardrailLines: - no free-form relation labels in active runtime
- no semantic drift between graph storage and UI rendering

assets.title: Canonical Workdir Contract Asset
assets.purpose: Single source of truth that the default phonowell project work directory is `.phonowell`.
contractLines: - each project stores runtime state, logs, generated artifacts, and replay evidence under `.phonowell`
- `.phonowell` is the stable working directory name unless an explicit project setting overrides its parent path
- persisted state path is scoped under the active project workdir
- logs, packet records, and generation diffs are project-local
- CLI, server, and WebUI must resolve the active project against `.phonowell`
- implementation should not scatter hidden runtime files at repository root once project mode is enabled

assets.title: Canonical Project Layer Asset
assets.purpose: Single source of truth for introducing a `project` layer above wells so users can manage multiple phonowell projects.
contractLines: - each project owns its own workdir, state, assets, and generated artifacts
- active project selection controls which `.phonowell` workspace is mounted
- switching project must fully swap runtime state instead of mixing assets across projects
- deleting project must remove only that project's managed runtime data
guardrailLines: - no cross-project hidden state bleed
- no shared active graph between unrelated projects

assets.title: Canonical Generator Diff Contract Asset
assets.purpose: Single source of truth that generation must consume asset-management diffs so the generator updates existing artifacts incrementally instead of fully regenerating every time.
contractLines: - each generate cycle computes an asset-management diff against the latest accepted or latest generated artifact state
- generator receives the diff as explicit context
- full regeneration is an explicit fallback, not the default path
guardrailLines: - no blind full rewrite on every generate
- no generator call without visible diff evidence once prior artifact state exists

assets.title: Canonical Asset Layering Contract Asset
assets.purpose: Single source of truth that active assets must distinguish hard contracts from default policy and reference guidance.
contractLines: 4. runtime and UI should expose asset layer so users can distinguish hard rules from guidance
guardrailLines: - no mixing mandatory contract and optional guidance without visible layer
- no reference asset treated as hard gate without explicit promotion

assets.title: Canonical V1 Delivery Asset
assets.purpose: Single source of truth for V1 scope, UX, event semantics, runtime contract, and milestone.
contractLines: - mode: low-UI assistant workspace with progressive disclosure
- continue loop or review requested checkpoint

assets.title: Canonical UI Observability Contract Asset
assets.purpose: Single source of truth that the WebUI must expose the key phonowell control signals instead of hiding them behind implementation internals.
contractLines: 2. visibility is glanceable in default mode
3. observability should not increase normal user action count
guardrailLines: - no hidden schema/version state during active development
- no gate-only signals trapped in CLI output

assets.title: Mimikit Reference Asset
assets.purpose: Reference source for proven orchestration and OpenAI/LLM interaction patterns that can be reused in `phonowell` with minimal adaptation cost.
guardrailLines: - do not copy unrelated modules
- do not import full architecture as-is
- do not introduce compatibility layers that violate phonowell simplification

assets.title: React 19 Reference Asset
assets.purpose: Reference baseline for implementing the WebUI graph interaction layer with stable, mature React capabilities and minimal custom uncertainty.
contractLines: 1. component and rendering model choices for canvas-first UI
2. state update patterns suitable for asset graph interaction
3. integration baseline for V1 delivery implementation
guardrailLines: - no framework lock beyond current V1 needs
- no feature adoption without ROI to canonical V1 goals

assets.title: Codex Reference Asset
assets.purpose: Reference tool pattern for fast implementation and verification loops while keeping `phonowell` core model vendor-neutral.
contractLines: 1. task execution patterns for iterative implementation
3. fast feedback loops for build/verify cycles
guardrailLines: - no tool-specific lock-in for core product logic
- no direct coupling to one vendor protocol in domain model

assets.title: Visualization-First Reference Asset
assets.purpose: Reference principle to prioritize visual expression over verbose text for understanding project structure and decisions.
contractLines: 1. prefer visual graph/card outputs for asset relationships and state
2. compress long textual explanations into visual summaries when possible
3. surface key status through glanceable visual cues
guardrailLines: - no decorative visuals without decision value
- no visual complexity that increases cognitive load

assets.title: Engineering Execution Reference Asset
assets.purpose: Unified engineering execution reference to keep delivery deterministic, low-friction, and non-blocking for users.
contractLines: 1. use explicit code implementation to remove uncertainty; delegate only inherently LLM-only tasks to LLM
2. default language/runtime for iteration: TypeScript + direct tsx startup
3. require all user-facing questions to be closed in dry-run; generate stage remains execution-only
4. keep implementation verifiable through contracts and observable run evidence
guardrailLines: - no compile-first requirement in normal dev iteration
- no LLM-first replacement for deterministic engineering logic
- no questioning users during generate stage
